name: @memberjunction/ai-bridge-ringcentral
description: MemberJunction: RingCentral Realtime Bridge driver (telephony). Connects the realtime agent engine to a phone call over a registered SIP softphone (ringcentral-softphone / SIP-TLS + RTP-SRTP) — the only RingCentral transport carrying bidirectional call audio (the WebSocket Call Streaming product is receive-only). Full-duplex audio in/out, outbound dial + inbound INVITE answer, DTMF send/receive, and blind transfer — via an injectable telephony call SDK seam. Audio only: no video, no screen, no Meeting Controls.
author: MemberJunction.com
//optionalDependencies: OPTIONAL PEER SDKs (CLAUDE rule 8, category 2): the package never statically imports these. The duplex SIP-softphone path loads `ringcentral-softphone` (a headless server-side SIP/RTP softphone — the only RingCentral product offering bidirectional call audio) + its `werift-rtp` peer (RtpPacket/RtpHeader/SrtpSession, not re-exported by the softphone package) lazily in createRingCentralSoftphone(); `@ringcentral/sdk` remains for the legacy Call-Control REST client. So the package builds and unit-tests with NONE of them installed (the SDK seams are injected with fakes). Loaded only when the RingCentral provider is configured at deployment.
